patches.hypothesis: The error is caused by the fact that the substring method is being called with a start index that is equal to the length of the string, which results in an out-of-bounds exception. This happens because the code is not properly checking for the end of the string before attempting to parse the imaginary character.

The error is caused by the `substring` method being called with a `startIndex` that is equal to the length of the string, which results in an index out of bounds exception. The bug is caused by the fact that the `substring` method is called with the `endIndex` parameter set to the length of the string, which is not correct. The `endIndex` parameter should be set to the length of the string minus one.

The error is caused by the `source.substring(startIndex, endIndex)` method call, which throws a `StringIndexOutOfBoundsException` when the `endIndex` is greater than the length of the string. This occurs when the `source` string is too short to contain the imaginary character.
